course_title: Python Learning Path: From Fundamentals to Advanced
course_description: A comprehensive, structured course for mastering Python from zero to advanced concepts, designed for software engineers and developers.

lessons.title: Installing Python and Setting Up Development Environment
lessons.objectives: Install Python 3.11+, configure IDE (VS Code/PyCharm), understand virtual environments with venv and pip.

lessons.title: First Steps: Hello World and Basic Syntax
lessons.objectives: Write your first Python script, understand indentation rules, comments, and basic program structure.

lessons.title: Variables, Data Types, and Type System
lessons.objectives: Learn about dynamic typing, basic types (int, float, str, bool), type conversion, and type hints introduction.

lessons.title: Basic Input/Output and String Formatting
lessons.objectives: Use print() and input(), format strings with f-strings, .format(), and % operator.

lessons.title: Conditional Statements: if, elif, else
lessons.objectives: Master branching logic, comparison operators, and nested conditionals.

lessons.title: Loops: while and for
lessons.objectives: Iterate with while loops and for loops, understand range(), and loop control (break, continue, else).

lessons.title: Logical Operators and Boolean Expressions
lessons.objectives: Combine conditions with and, or, not; understand truthiness and operator precedence.

lessons.title: Match-Case Statements (Python 3.10+)
lessons.objectives: Use structural pattern matching for complex conditional logic.

modules.title: Data Structures: Lists, Tuples, Dictionaries, Sets

lessons.title: Lists: Creation, Indexing, and Basic Operations
lessons.objectives: Create lists, access elements, slicing, common methods (append, extend, insert, remove, pop).

lessons.title: List Comprehensions and Advanced Manipulation
lessons.objectives: Write concise list comprehensions, filter, map, and sort lists.

lessons.title: Tuples: Immutable Sequences
lessons.objectives: Understand tuple immutability, packing/unpacking, and when to use tuples vs lists.

lessons.title: Dictionaries: Key-Value Mappings
lessons.objectives: Create dictionaries, access values, iterate over keys/values/items, and common methods.

lessons.title: Dictionary Comprehensions and Dict Methods
lessons.objectives: Build dictionaries with comprehensions, use get(), setdefault(), update(), and understand defaultdict.

lessons.title: Sets: Unordered Unique Collections
lessons.objectives: Create sets, perform set operations (union, intersection, difference), and understand set comprehensions.

lessons.title: Defining and Calling Functions
lessons.objectives: Write functions with def, understand parameters and return values, scope rules (LEGB).

lessons.title: Arguments: Positional, Keyword, Default, and *args/**kwargs
lessons.objectives: Master flexible function signatures, unpacking arguments, and variable-length parameters.

lessons.title: Lambda Functions and Higher-Order Functions
lessons.objectives: Use lambda expressions, pass functions as arguments, and understand map/filter/reduce.

lessons.title: Docstrings, Type Hints, and Function Annotations
lessons.objectives: Write professional docstrings (Google/NumPy style), add type hints, and use typing module.

lessons.title: Modules and Packages
lessons.objectives: Organize code into modules, create packages with __init__.py, and use import statements correctly.

lessons.title: The if __name__ == '__main__' Pattern
lessons.objectives: Make files executable as scripts and importable as modules.

lessons.title: Classes and Objects: Basics of OOP
lessons.objectives: Define classes, create objects (instances), understand __init__ and self.

lessons.title: Instance Variables, Class Variables, and Methods
lessons.objectives: Distinguish between instance and class attributes, define instance/class/static methods.

lessons.title: Inheritance and Method Resolution Order (MRO)
lessons.objectives: Create subclass relationships, use super(), understand multiple inheritance and MRO.

lessons.title: Encapsulation: Public, Private, and Protected
lessons.objectives: Use naming conventions (_protected, __private), understand name mangling.

lessons.title: Properties, Getters, and Setters
lessons.objectives: Implement computed properties with @property, create getter/setter decorators.

lessons.title: Special (Dunder) Methods
lessons.objectives: Implement __str__, __repr__, __len__, __getitem__, __setitem__, and other magic methods.

lessons.title: Polymorphism and Duck Typing
lessons.objectives: Understand polymorphism in Python, embrace duck typing, and use abstract base classes (ABC).

lessons.title: Exceptions: try, except, else, finally
lessons.objectives: Catch and handle exceptions, understand the exception hierarchy, use else and finally blocks.

lessons.title: Raising Exceptions and Custom Exceptions
lessons.objectives: Raise exceptions with raise, define custom exception classes by inheriting from Exception.

lessons.title: Context Managers and the with Statement
lessons.objectives: Use with for resource management, create custom context managers with __enter__ and __exit__.

lessons.title: Exception Chaining and Best Practices
lessons.objectives: Preserve traceback with raise from, follow Pythonic error handling patterns.

lessons.title: Reading and Writing Text Files
lessons.objectives: Open files with open(), use read/write modes, context managers, and encoding parameters.

lessons.title: Working with CSV and JSON Data
lessons.objectives: Parse CSV files with csv module, serialize/deserialize JSON with json module.

lessons.title: Pickle and Binary Serialization
lessons.objectives: Use pickle for Python object serialization, understand security implications.

lessons.title: Working with Paths: os.path and pathlib
lessons.objectives: Manipulate file paths with os.path and the modern pathlib API.

lessons.title: File System Operations
lessons.objectives: List directories, check file existence, create/remove files and directories.

lessons.title: Iterators and the Iterator Protocol
lessons.objectives: Understand iter() and next(), create custom iterator classes, use iterators efficiently.

lessons.title: Generators and yield
lessons.objectives: Write generator functions, use yield for lazy evaluation, understand generator expressions.

lessons.title: Decorators: Functions That Wrap Functions
lessons.objectives: Create function decorators, use @ syntax, understand closure and functools.wraps.

lessons.title: Contextlib and Advanced Context Managers
lessons.objectives: Use contextmanager decorator, suppress, redirect_stdout, and other utilities.

lessons.title: Descriptors and the Descriptor Protocol
lessons.objectives: Implement __get__, __set__, __delete__ for custom attribute behavior.

lessons.title: Metaclasses and Class Creation
lessons.objectives: Understand type(), create custom metaclasses, and know when to use them (rarely).

lessons.title: Threading: Running Code Concurrently
lessons.objectives: Create threads with threading.Thread, understand GIL, and use thread synchronization (Lock, RLock, Event).

lessons.title: Multiprocessing: True Parallel Execution
lessons.objectives: Spawn processes with multiprocessing, share data with Queue and Pipe, bypass GIL for CPU-bound tasks.

lessons.title: Asyncio: Asynchronous I/O
lessons.objectives: Write async functions with async/await, use Event Loop, understand coroutines and tasks.

lessons.title: Concurrent Futures: ThreadPoolExecutor and ProcessPoolExecutor
lessons.objectives: Simplify concurrent code with high-level executors, map tasks to pools.

lessons.title: Choosing the Right Concurrency Model
lessons.objectives: Decide between threading, multiprocessing, and asyncio based on I/O vs CPU bound tasks.

lessons.title: Writing Tests with unittest and pytest
lessons.objectives: Write unit tests, use assertions, fixtures, and parametrize tests with pytest.

lessons.title: Test-Driven Development (TDD) Workflow
lessons.objectives: Follow Red-Green-Refactor cycle, write tests before implementation.

lessons.title: Debugging with pdb and IDE Debuggers
lessons.objectives: Set breakpoints, step through code, inspect variables, use post-mortem debugging.

lessons.title: Logging: The Right Way to Print
lessons.objectives: Configure logging module, use different log levels, format logs, and file handlers.

lessons.title: Code Quality: PEP 8, Linters, and Formatters
lessons.objectives: Follow PEP 8, use flake8/pylint, and auto-format with black and isort.

lessons.title: Type Checking with mypy
lessons.objectives: Run static type checking, understand common mypy errors, improve code reliability.

lessons.title: Profiling and Performance Optimization
lessons.objectives: Profile code with cProfile, timeit, and line_profiler; identify bottlenecks.

lessons.title: Packaging and Distributing Python Packages
lessons.objectives: Create setup.py/pyproject.toml, build distributions, and publish to PyPI (optional).
